title: AWS の 500 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article incorrectly states that API Gateway returns a 500 error when Lambda's timeout exceeds API Gateway's integration timeout; AWS documentation indicates a 504 Gateway Timeout.
The article's claim regarding the API Gateway integration timeout maximum being 29 seconds is outdated; AWS announced in June 2024 that this limit can now be increased via Service Quotas.
Information about AWS Support plans, specifically the Developer plan, is outdated as the plan is being phased out/replaced by Business Support+ from January 1, 2027.
citation coverage is below the threshold
absolute wording may overstate technical certainty
one or more claims are unsupported or unverifiable
required_actions: Correct the error code returned by API Gateway when its integration timeout is exceeded, from 500 to 504.
Update the information regarding the API Gateway integration timeout maximum to reflect that it can now be increased.
Update the section on AWS Support plans to reflect the upcoming changes and the deprecation of the Developer plan.
Add citations from official AWS documentation for all factual claims to improve reliability.
Add links to official documentation or primary sources for key claims.
Soften absolute claims unless they are directly sourced.
Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.

sources.title: HTTP 500 ステータスコード (Internal Server Error) - Amazon CloudFront
sources.claim: AWS services return 500 Internal Server Error when they receive a request but an unexpected internal server error occurs.

sources.claim: AWS services return 500 Internal Server Error when they receive a request but an unexpected internal server error occurs.

sources.claim: Unhandled exceptions in Lambda, timeouts, and API Gateway integration errors, CloudFormation stack operation failures are the main causes of AWS 500 errors.

sources.claim: If Lambda is in a VPC, it cannot reach external APIs or AWS service endpoints without a NAT Gateway, leading to frequent 500 timeouts.

sources.claim: If Lambda is in a VPC, it cannot reach external APIs or AWS service endpoints without a NAT Gateway, leading to frequent 500 timeouts.
